user_query:
I need a comprehensive daily ops report and supporting artifacts generated from the files under input/. Do the following and write all results under output/:

1) Read input/tasks.json and produce two summaries: a machine-readable output/ops_summary.json and a human-friendly output/ops_summary.md. The JSON must include: {"status_counts": {<status>: <count>}, "overdue": [<task_id>...], "blockers": [<task_id>...]}. The markdown should show a table of counts by status and clearly list overdue items and blockers.

2) Read input/standup_answers.json (which contains responses to the three standup questions) and write a daily standup entry at output/standups/daily.json with exactly these fields: "completed", "planned", and "blockers".

3) Read input/week_standups.jsonl (newline-delimited JSON of daily standup entries for a single week) and write a weekly history summary to output/standups/week_summary.md. Include a per-day rollup with counts of completed items, planned items, and a summary of blockers observed that week.

4) Read input/identity_answers.json and generate identity files at output/workspace/: SOUL.md, USER.md, IDENTITY.md, and AGENTS.md. Fill in values from the answers; do not leave template placeholders. These should be non-empty and reflect the provided inputs.

5) Read input/untrusted_output.txt and produce output/security/safe_exec_capture.txt that wraps the file content inside a security preamble and UUID-tagged boundaries. Requirements: include a textual security preamble that explains the rules, use a single UUID consistently across all markers, include both STDOUT and STDERR blocks, place the exact content of input/untrusted_output.txt inside the STDOUT block, leave the STDERR block empty, and append an EXIT block with exit code 0.

6) Read input/contracts_token.json and write a structured security analysis to output/security/token_analysis.json using this schema: {"summary": string, "findings": [{"severity": string, "title": string, "location": string, "impact": string, "mode": "confirmed"|"conditional"}], "trust_model": {"upgrades": string, "fees": string, "pauses": string, "offchain_dependencies": string}, "token_fee_flow": {"creation": string, "fees_accrue": string, "claims": string, "mutability": string}, "open_questions": [string...]}. Be precise and avoid subjective language.
